subject: FW:  INVITATION:   Answer the call of the Pipes and Drums!!
body:
From: Ellen Snibbe Baker [mailto:ellensnibbebaker@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, December 11, 2007 12:33 AM
To: Ellen Snibbe Baker
Subject: INVITATION: Answer the call of the Pipes and Drums!!

 

Dear Friends:

 

We hope you and your family will join us on Sunday, December 16th at 3pm
for the thrilling "Pipes of Christmas" Concert, our annual Musical
Scholarship Fundraiser produced by the Clan Currie Society.

 

Please see information below for tickets, which are available via SMART
TIX. 

 

With regards for a wonderful holiday season,

 

 

Suzanne J. Currie

Host Committee

 

________________________________

An offer from SmartTix <http://mail2.smarttix.com/t/64053/198598/578/0/>


Answer the call of the Pipes and Drums 

________________________________

November 26, 2007 

TeaserImg <http://mail2.smarttix.com/t/64053/198598/578/0/> 


"...a majestic seasonal experience..." 

"...a unique sound of power and glory..." 

"...an event to be savored and cherished..." 

>From the bold sounds of the pipes and drums, and the harmonious blending
of brass, strings and percussion, to the poetic and lyrical words that
complement them, all filling the beautiful Madison Avenue Presbyterian
Church, The Pipes Of Christmas is a festival for the soul. The concert
features the music of Christmas accompanied by a selection of readings
taken from the Celtic literature of Scotland, Ireland, and Wales. 

Featured performers include Andrew Weir from the film "Braveheart,"
Metropolitan Opera baritone Mark Delavan, Scottish Country Dance trio -
Local Hero, the Solid Brass ensemble, Scottish harpist Jennifer Port and
the Kevin Ray Blandford Memorial Pipe Band of Redlands, CA. 

Premium VIP seats available. Produced by the Clan Currie Society,
proceeds benefit the Society's scholarship program. 

The Pipes Of Christmas gratefully acknowledges the generous support
received from the Grand Summit Hotel, Summit, NJ, Glenfarclas Single
Malt Scotch Whisky, and Walkers Shortbread. 

Sunday, December 16, 2007 - 3:00 PM 

SEE A PREVIEW AT www.scottishamerican.org
<http://mail2.smarttix.com/t/64053/198598/579/0/>

body_html:
From: Ellen Snibbe Baker [mailto:ellensnibbebaker@yahoo.com]
Sent: Tuesday, December 11, 2007 12:33 AM
To: Ellen Snibbe Baker
Subject: INVITATION: Answer the call of the Pipes and Drums!!

 

Dear Friends:

 

We hope you and your family will join us on Sunday, December 16th at 3pm for the thrilling "Pipes of Christmas" Concert, our annual Musical Scholarship Fundraiser produced by the Clan Currie Society.

 

Please see information below for tickets, which are available via SMART TIX. 

 

With regards for a wonderful holiday season,

 

 

Suzanne J. Currie

Host Committee

 

Answer the call of the Pipes and Drums

November 26, 2007


"...a majestic seasonal experience..."

"...a unique sound of power and glory..."

"...an event to be savored and cherished..."

From the bold sounds of the pipes and drums, and the harmonious blending of brass, strings and percussion, to the poetic and lyrical words that complement them, all filling the beautiful Madison Avenue Presbyterian Church, The Pipes Of Christmas is a festival for the soul. The concert features the music of Christmas accompanied by a selection of readings taken from the Celtic literature of Scotland, Ireland, and Wales.

Featured performers include Andrew Weir from the film "Braveheart," Metropolitan Opera baritone Mark Delavan, Scottish Country Dance trio - Local Hero, the Solid Brass ensemble, Scottish harpist Jennifer Port and the Kevin Ray Blandford Memorial Pipe Band of Redlands, CA.

Premium VIP seats available. Produced by the Clan Currie Society, proceeds benefit the Society's scholarship program.

The Pipes Of Christmas gratefully acknowledges the generous support received from the Grand Summit Hotel, Summit, NJ, Glenfarclas Single Malt Scotch Whisky, and Walkers Shortbread.

Sunday, December 16, 2007 - 3:00 PM

SEE A PREVIEW AT www.scottishamerican.org

How To Order

  • On Line: www.smarttix.com
  • By Phone: 212-868-4444

Venue Information

Madison Avenue Presbyterian
921 Madison Avenue
New York, NY 10021
(73rd/74th Street)

 

 

Terms and Limitations: All sales final. No refunds or exchanges.
